title: What does safe with money feel like in my body?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for mapping the felt experience of financial safety, not just the idea of it.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 21 of the MONEY program, the final session of Phase 1. Use to move from cognitive understanding of financial safety to a specific, embodied description of what it actually feels like.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 21 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a period of money work to consolidate and map the change in your body, or any time "financial safety" feels like an abstract concept rather than something you've touched.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 21 of the MONEY program. The prompts ask for a body-level description of safety with money, not a financial plan or a belief statement. What the shoulders do. What the jaw does. What the stomach does. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
When I imagine my finances as completely settled and stable, where does my body change first?
Start in the body. Chest, jaw, shoulders, stomach. Write what shifts.
What does financial stress feel like in my body, by contrast?
Name the specific physical signals — not "anxious" but the actual sensation of anxiety in your body.
What does "safe with money" feel like in my body?
Describe it physically, in as much detail as you can. If you can't find the feeling yet, write what you imagine it would feel like.
Have I felt that physical safety with money, even briefly? When?
Write the specific moment. The day, the balance, the detail.
What would change in my daily life if that body-feeling was the default?
Write the small practical changes: how you'd open your banking app, what you'd stop doing, what would get easier.
Adapted from Day 21 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
